column1: # manifest.tsv — Plane 1: GLOBAL system CLIs only (installed via the system package
# manager, never vendored). sprout validates presence and, with --install-clis or the
# wizard's CLI picker, installs the missing ones. TAB-separated.
# Columns:  name  brew_pkg  pacman_pkg  apt_pkg  dnf_pkg  zypper_pkg  phase  types  desc
#   managers: brew(macOS) pacman(Arch) apt(Debian·Ubuntu) dnf(Fedora) zypper(openSUSE)
#           but never auto-installed/preselected (only installed if you pick it).
#   pkg:    a `-` means "not packaged for that manager" → sprout prints a manual hint.
#           an `npm:<pkg>` spec installs via `npm install -g <pkg>` on any OS.
# NOTE: project-level dev tools (@playwright/test, biome, eslint, …) do NOT belong here —
# they are installed per project by the chosen JS package manager (pnpm/npm/yarn/bun).
# The GLOBAL @playwright/cli (binary `playwright-cli`, for coding agents) is different and
# DOES live on PATH, so it is listed below as optional. See cli.md for the full breakdown.
# Debian/Ubuntu wart: `fd` ships as `fd-find` (binary `fdfind`) and `bat` as `batcat`;
# sprout symlinks them to fd/bat in ~/.local/bin so the expected command actually works.
